character_name: Old man on the saklya roof
physical_anchor: Wiry, small-framed Caucasian elder with a sun-cured, deeply lined face half-hidden in a grey beard, stooped shoulders wrapped in a worn sheepskin over a faded beshmet. A habitual gesture of laying one hand flat on his chest before speaking, and a slow, careful tread as if every footstep were measured against a sleeping household.
vocal_quality: Low, dry, gravel-soft baritone pitched just above a whisper, with a slow measured tempo and long pauses between short finished sentences.
social_presence: Occupies a room through stillness rather than authority — a quiet, watchful warmth that makes guests lower their own voices, the kind of invisibility that hides a householder who has already decided what he will and will not say.
